payload.title: Budget Watch October 2023 Flash Report
payload.description: Summary of September 2023 financial results and YTD September 2023 results against Mid-Year Forecast, plus regional economy report and August 2023 ridership data

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: TWU Local 100 contract impact on September 2023
tool_args.raw_text: September actuals reflect the payment of essential worker bonuses and wage increases as stipulated in the recently approved TWU Local 100 contract.

payload.claim_text: September actuals reflect the payment of essential worker bonuses and wage increases as stipulated in the recently approved TWU Local 100 contract.

tool_args.label: Heavy rainfall impacted September 2023 passenger revenues
tool_args.raw_text: In September, passenger revenues fell below the midpoint, likely due to heavy rainfall on September 29th, which totaling over 5 inches, and calendar issues.

payload.claim_text: In September, passenger revenues fell below the midpoint, likely due to heavy rainfall on September 29th, which totaling over 5 inches, and calendar issues.

tool_args.label: Passenger revenues remain significantly below pre-pandemic levels
tool_args.raw_text: YTD September passenger revenues were down $1.3 billion (28%) compared with YTD September 2019 and September passenger revenues were $147 million (31%) below September 2019.

payload.claim_text: YTD September passenger revenues were down $1.3 billion (28%) compared with YTD September 2019 and September passenger revenues were $147 million (31%) below September 2019.

tool_args.label: October 2023 Payroll Mobility Tax - unfavorable variance
NYC Employment YoY change August 2023 vs August 2022
Employment vs Ridership long-term trend
tool_args.raw_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. The COVID pandemic has altered these trajectories: compared with January 2017, August employment is 6.1% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 37.8% lower.

payload.claim_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. Compared with January 2017, August employment is 6.1% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 37.8% lower.

tool_args.label: Ridership data preliminary
tool_args.raw_text: NOTE: Ridership data are preliminary and subject to revision as well as adjustments warranted by annual audit review.

payload.gap_text: Ridership data are preliminary and subject to revision as well as adjustments warranted by annual audit review.
payload.missing_information: Ridership data may be revised after annual audit review
